message.content: @startuml title Interactions between the Control Plane (master node) and the Worker Node skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center skinparam participantStyle rectangle hide footbox ' Define colors based on legend colorDef controlPlane #A9D0F5 ' Light blue for master components colorDef workerNode #B6E8C3 ' Greenish for kubelet/proxy/node agent colorDef systemSetup #FFCCCB ' Reddish tint for setup steps colorDef configSteps #FFE4B1 ' Orangey/tan for configuration files/objects colorDef deployment #FFFACD ' Lemon chiffon for deployments actor "Georgina" as Georgina participant "API-Server" as APIServer <> participant "etcd" as etcd <> participant "Controller Manager\n(kube-controller-manager)\n(contains Deployment Controller,\nReplicaSet Controller)" as CMgr <> participant "Scheduler\n(kube-scheduler)\nschedules pods to nodes." as Scheduler <> participant "Power Operator\ncustom controller managing power profiles.\nwatches PowerConfig CRs & reconciles state by updating Kubelet's CPUManagerPolicy via ConfigMap or direct API call if supported; triggers rescheduling of affected Pods through eviction/recreation mechanism controlled under strict conditions defined within each profile spec field like ‘enforce’ flag set true/false accordingly depending upon desired behavior expected out from this operator during runtime environment where multiple conflicting policies might exist simultaneously causing potential race condition issues unless handled properly using distributed locking mechanisms provided either natively inside Kubernetes itself such leader election pattern implemented across all replicas running same instance type together forming quorum majority vote decision making process ensuring consistency guarantees met always even though split brain scenario could occur occasionally due network partition failures happening randomly anywhere anytime without warning signs beforehand predictably preventable ahead time proactively
